title: 基础文件能力
summary: Implement Phase 2 as a tests-first expansion of the existing Rust action/config/log core, then wire the same action schema through CLI, Tauri/Vue preferences, and Swift FinderSync. Keep the current Tauri 2 + Vue 3 + TypeScript + pnpm + Vite baseline; add no frontend dependency unless the UI state becomes complex enough to justify Pinia or icons need lucide-vue-next.

ActionResult.payload: serde_json::Value for clipboard text or file info details

DangerousConfirmationConfig.action_ids: Vec<String>, default all dangerous action ids

dangerous_confirmation_default: Default config requires confirmation for file.move_to_trash and file.delete_permanently. CLI/core must reject dangerous actions unless confirmed_dangerous is true when confirmation is enabled. FinderSync shows native confirmation before invoking CLI and passes --confirmed-dangerous only after user confirms. Tauri preferences expose the setting, default on.
macos_boundaries: Rust core owns path transformations, metadata/file info, temp-dir-testable folder operations, and final dispatch errors.

Move to Trash should use a small macOS-compatible implementation boundary; prefer an adapter/helper that can be tested with temp fixtures and can later swap to NSWorkspace/FileManager behavior if needed.
Permanent delete uses std::fs remove_file/remove_dir_all only after explicit confirmation and only on selected paths.
Clipboard writes should be isolated behind a helper so tests can assert returned payload text without depending on a user clipboard.
FinderSync only builds menus, collects selectedItemURLs, confirms dangerous actions with native UI, and shells out to sright-cli.
verification_commands: xcodebuild -project native/macos/sRightNative.xcodeproj -scheme SRightFinderSync -configuration Debug build CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO

known_risks: FinderSync confirmation UI and sandbox behavior need packaged-app smoke testing.
Rust config, TypeScript interfaces, and Swift Decodable structs can drift unless schema changes are made in one task.
Trash implementation may need a macOS-specific adapter dependency or native bridge after initial temp-dir tests.

plan_check.coverage: covers all Phase 2 requested actions, dangerous confirmation, logging, CLI, Tauri/Vue, and FinderSync
plan_check.pressure_pass: FinderSync must not reimplement file operations; it only maps menu item representedObject to action id, confirms if config marks action dangerous, and invokes CLI with paths.
plan_check.collision_detection: non-blocking scan found only Ralph status/wave files and this scratch plan; no same-milestone plan.json collision found
